target.title: [AI] fix(memory): auto-fix providerKey mismatch from CLI index --force
target.body:
## Summary

Running `openclaw memory index --force` writes a `providerKey` based on the initialized provider's `cacheKeyData` (which may include `baseUrl`, `headers`, `dimensions`). When the runtime manager later initializes with different `cacheKeyData` (e.g. the CLI lacked runtime context), the `providerKey` comparison fails and `memory_search` is permanently disabled with "index provider settings changed" until meta is manually rewritten.

Now `refreshIndexIdentityDirty` auto-fixes providerKey-only mismatches: when the identity state is "mismatched" solely because `providerKey` differs (provider id + model still match), it updates `meta.providerKey` to the current value and re-evaluates identity, restoring `memory_search` without requiring a full reindex.

This mirrors the self-heal pattern established by #91897 for missing-identity FTS-only chunks.

- Problem: CLI `openclaw memory index --force` writes stale `providerKey` (e.g. `SHA256({"provider":"none","model":"fts-only"})` before provider init), causing runtime `memory_search` to be permanently disabled with "index provider settings changed"
- Solution: In `refreshIndexIdentityDirty`, when provider is initialized and the only mismatch is `providerKey`, automatically update meta with the current `providerKey` and re-evaluate identity
- What changed: `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager.ts` (+21 lines in `refreshIndexIdentityDirty`), new test file `manager.providerkey-auto-fix.test.ts` (2 tests)
- What did NOT change: Identity check logic in `resolveMemoryIndexIdentityState`, CLI code, config surfaces, default values


## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix

## Scope

- [x] Memory / storage

## Linked Issue

- Refs #91902 (providerKey mismatch auto-fix; the scopeHash issue mentioned in the issue is separate and not addressed here)

## Motivation

Issue #91902 reports that `openclaw memory index --force` disables `memory_search` with "index built with a different provider". Root cause: CLI creates `MemoryIndexManager` with `purpose="cli"` and no `providerResult`, so `computeProviderKey()` returns `SHA256({"provider":"none","model":"fts-only"})` before provider initialization. After `ensureProviderInitialized()` runs during sync, the `providerKey` is recomputed with the real provider's `cacheKeyData` — but this new key is written into meta. When a runtime manager later starts and its `cacheKeyData` differs (e.g. different `dimensions`, `headers`, or `baseUrl` resolved differently), the `providerKey` comparison fails and search is permanently disabled.

The fix is minimal and additive: when the only identity mismatch is `providerKey` (provider id + model still match), auto-update the stale key instead of blocking search.

## Changes

**File**: `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager.ts`

In `refreshIndexIdentityDirty`, after computing the identity state:
- If state is "mismatched" with reason "index provider settings changed", provider is initialized, and `this.providerKey` is available
- Read current meta, check if only `providerKey` differs
- Update `meta.providerKey` to `this.providerKey` and write back
- Re-evaluate identity state with the updated meta

**File**: `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager.providerkey-auto-fix.test.ts` (new)

2 tests:
1. Auto-fixes stale providerKey written by CLI and allows search to proceed
2. Does not auto-fix when provider id also differs (genuine mismatched)

No new imports, no config changes, no default surface changes.

## Real Behavior Proof

- Behavior addressed: `memory_search` is permanently disabled with "index provider settings changed" after CLI `openclaw memory index --force` writes a `providerKey` that differs from what the runtime manager computes
- Real environment tested: Linux, Node.js 22.22, local checkout on branch `fix/memory-providerkey-mismatch-91902` at commit `79de87945`
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager.providerkey-auto-fix.test.ts --run
```

- Evidence after fix:

```console
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager.providerkey-auto-fix.test.ts --run

 RUN  v4.1.7 /home/0668000363/requre-an/MAAS-MR/memory-work-main/07 Open Source/02-projects/openclaw

 ✓ |extension-memory| extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager.providerkey-auto-fix.test.ts (2 tests) 261ms

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  2 passed (2)
```

- Observed result after fix: When `meta.providerKey` is stale (e.g. `SHA256({"provider":"none","model":"fts-only"})` from CLI pre-init), `refreshIndexIdentityDirty` detects the providerKey-only mismatch, updates `meta.providerKey` to the current computed value, and re-evaluates identity as "valid". Search proceeds normally. When provider id also differs (genuine mismatch), the auto-fix does not apply and search remains blocked.
- What was not tested: Live CLI `openclaw memory index --force` followed by runtime `memory_search` with a real openai-compatible provider. The `cacheKeyData`-derived `providerKey` difference is simulated by writing a stale FTS-only providerKey into meta.

## Risks

- **Low risk**: The auto-fix only activates when (1) provider is initialized, (2) identity is mismatched, (3) the mismatch reason is exactly "index provider settings changed", and (4) `meta.providerKey` differs from the current `this.providerKey`. If provider id or model also differ, the fix does not apply.
- **Additive**: The change only adds a repair path; it does not change the identity check logic in `resolveMemoryIndexIdentityState`.
- **No config/default surface changes**: No new config keys, env vars, or defaults introduced.
- **Scope limitation**: The `scopeHash` mismatch issue also mentioned in #91902 is not addressed here — it requires a separate fix for `normalizeMemoryMultimodalSettings` default handling.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Users who previously saw "memory search is paused because the memory index was built with a different embedding provider/model/settings" after running `openclaw memory index --force` will now have search automatically restored on the next runtime sync or search, provided the provider id and model match.

## Security Impact

- [x] New permissions/capabilities? No
- [x] Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- [x] New/changed network calls? No
- [x] Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- [x] Data access scope changed? No

## Human Verification

- Verified scenarios: Stale providerKey auto-fix (test 1), provider id mismatch no-fix (test 2), existing self-heal and reindex-state tests still pass
- Edge cases checked: Provider not initialized (auto-fix skipped), provider id also differs (auto-fix skipped)
- What you did NOT verify: Live CLI + runtime end-to-end with real provider

## Compatibility / Migration

- [x] Backward compatible? Yes
- [x] Config/env changes? No
- [x] Migration needed? No

## AI Assistance 🤖

- **AI-assisted**: Yes
- **Human confirmed understanding of code changes**: Yes
- **AI prompts / session excerpts**: Issue #91902 analysis → source code exploration of `computeProviderKey`, `resolveMemoryIndexIdentityState`, `refreshIndexIdentityDirty`, CLI `runMemoryIndex` path → designed auto-fix in `refreshIndexIdentityDirty`

---

Refs #91902
target.author: xydt-tanshanshan
